title: QUIC RFC 9000 ACK_ECN Frame Codec Verification
verification_method: Requirement-home execution, deterministic frame-codec fuzz coverage, BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation, and SpecTrace render/check for the bounded ACK_ECN frame-codec slice.
scope: This verification covers the supported ACK and ACK_ECN frame-codec floor: accepting ACK frame types 0x02 and 0x03, requiring all three ECN count fields for type 0x03, rejecting unsupported ACK type values and serializer type/count mismatches, encoding each ECN count as a QUIC variable-length integer, and preserving parsed cumulative count values.
expected_result: Focused tests prove positive and negative ACK_ECN frame-codec behavior, deterministic fuzz coverage exercises representative ACK/ACK_ECN shapes and truncation rejection, and the BenchmarkDotNet Dry suite executes representative parse and format hot-path cases without treating Dry output as a stable performance baseline.
procedure: Run the focused RFC 9000 ACK and ACK_ECN requirement-home tests for S19P3-0001, S19P3-0003, S19P3-0009, and S19P3P2 requirements.
Confirm deterministic ACK frame fuzz coverage remains linked for representative ACK/ACK_ECN round-trips and truncation rejection.
Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
Confirm that no receiver IP ECN access, sender validation, congestion response, runtime ACK scheduling, public API, or interop ECN claim is introduced.
preconditions: The frame codec receives a complete contiguous ACK frame payload.
The caller uses `QuicAckFrame.FrameType` to distinguish type 0x02 from type 0x03.
The caller uses `QuicEcnCounts` only for the three cumulative ACK_ECN counters and not as proof of endpoint ECN deployment.

supplemental_sections.heading: Fuzz And Benchmark Scope
supplemental_sections.content: The deterministic requirement-home fuzz proof covers representative ACK and ACK_ECN round-trips plus truncation rejection through the frame-codec entry point. The permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite covers ACK_ECN parsing and formatting alongside the existing frame-codec cases; Dry validation is harness evidence only and is not treated as a stable performance baseline.

status_summary: Passed locally on 2026-04-25. Focused ACK/ACK_ECN requirement-home tests passed 14/14, and the updated QuicFrameCodecBenchmarks Dry suite executed all 10 frame-codec cases including ACK_ECN parse and format. Dry benchmark results are harness evidence only and are not treated as stable performance baselines.
